description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Discovered during S7 (mutagen-wrd.9) end-to-end test driver authoring.

## Problem

`lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:411` — the production mix task constructs the MutationRunner input with a literal `test_modules: []`. `MutationLoop`'s per-site `Enum.each(test_modules, &ExUnit.Server.add_module/2)` is therefore a no-op against the empty list, so `ExUnit.run/0` inside the mutation pass reports zero tests run, and EVERY mutation is classified `:survived`.

## Reproduction

Run `mix mutagen --scope lib/foo.ex --tests test/foo_test.exs` against any module that has live mutators: `mutation.results[*].status` is uniformly `"survived"` regardless of whether the cited tests would actually kill those mutations.

## E2E driver workaround

The S7 driver's `mutation_collaborator/1` does an AST walk of each cited `_test.exs`, finds every `defmodule` block, and populates `test_modules` with `[{mod, %{async?: false, group: nil, parameterize: nil}}]` entries before delegating to `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1`. The production caller still bypasses this.

## Spec reference

`mutagen.mutation_pipeline` r3 (mutation classification semantics) and r5 (`:killed` vs `:survived` semantics) — these rules cannot be exercised by the production wire-up while every result is forced to `:survived`.

## Resolution

In `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` around line 411, derive `test_modules` from the resolved `TestFilter.files` (or equivalent) rather than hardcoding `[]`. Retire the `mutation_collaborator` fork in `end_to_end_test.exs` once the production code is fixed.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 end-to-end suite, where this was discovered)
- mutagen-wrd.11 (TestSelector exclude: [:test] fix — same wire-up surface)

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea.jsonl

Commit: 6cc625a
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.12/mix-mutagen-test-modules
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-mix-mutagen-test-modules
Based on: 258665a (epic/mutagen-wrd at mutagen-wrd.11 head, per orchestrator briefing)

Files touched:
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex — phase_mutation/7 now derives test_modules
  from test_filter.files via MutagenEx.TestModuleDiscovery.discover/1
  instead of hardcoding [].
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery.ex (NEW) — pure-AST walk of cited
  *_test.exs files; returns [{module, %{async?: false, group: nil,
  parameterize: nil}}]. Unreadable/unparseable files contribute zero
  entries without raising.
- test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs — retired the mutation_collaborator
  fork + its discover_test_modules/1 + alias_to_module/1 helpers per
  the ticket's Resolution clause; dispatch now points at
  MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1 directly. Moduledoc updated to match.
- test/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery_test.exs (NEW) — 12 unit tests.
- test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs — added a regression test that
  Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 hands MutationRunner a non-empty test_modules
  derived from test_filter.files. Would have failed pre-fix.

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.TestModuleDiscoveryTest (12 tests): empty input, single +
  multiple defmodules, multi-file ordering, dotted aliases, non-_test.exs
  files, nested defmodule blocks, missing/unparseable/no-defmodule files
  (each returns [] without raising), and the locked-in
  `%{async?: false, group: nil, parameterize: nil}` cfg shape.
- MutagenEx.JsonReporterTest "mix task hands MutationRunner a populated
  test_modules derived from test_filter.files (mutagen-wrd.12)" — uses
  the dispatch capture pattern to assert phase_mutation/7's input to
  the mutation collaborator.

Verification stubs flipped: none — mutagen.mutation_pipeline's existing
v2 stub (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs`) is the
recorded gate for r5; both v2 and the e2e suite remain `execute: true`
and continue to pass. No new stub block was added because the spec text
already covers the killed/survived contract this ticket restores; the
fix is a production-wiring repair, not a new behavioral invariant.

Smoke (mix compile --warnings-as-errors + mix test):
- default suite: 321 tests, 0 failures (was 308 in baseline; +13 from
  the new test_module_discovery_test + json_reporter regression test)
- mix test --only e2e_slow: 13 tests, 0 failures, 2 honest skips (the
  same two skips that existed pre-change: mutagen-wrd.13's ecto_user
  scenario and mutagen-wrd.15/.16's literal-mutator-on-decisions.ex
  scenario 2a). All eleven non-skipped e2e scenarios now go through
  the production MutationRunner.run/1, proving the production wire-up
  reproduces what the prior mutation_collaborator fork demonstrated.

Summary: `Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.phase_mutation/7` no longer hands
MutationRunner an empty `test_modules` list. The mix task now uses
`MutagenEx.TestModuleDiscovery.discover/1` (extracted from the e2e
driver's prior workaround) to walk each cited test file's AST for
`defmodule` blocks and package them in the `{module, %{async?: false,
group: nil, parameterize: nil}}` shape `ExUnit.Server.add_module/2`
consumes. With MutationLoop's per-site re-registration loop now
operating on a real list, the `:killed` vs `:survived` distinction
in `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r5` is observable through the production
pipeline for the first time. The e2e suite's `mutation_collaborator`
fork is gone — the production runner is what every scenario exercises.

title: DISCOVERY: mix mutagen passes hardcoded test_modules: [] to MutationRunner
